name: Creamed Oysters in Patty Shell
recipeIngredient: 1 Quart shucked Maryland standard oysters (with liquor)
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the oysters and reserve the liquor.
2. Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
3. Add chopped onion and celery to the melted butter; sauté until they are soft and golden brown, stirring occasionally.
4. Gradually add 2 tablespoons of flour to the pan, stirring constantly to prevent lumps.
5. Slowly stir in the reserved oyster liquor and milk, ensuring a smooth mixture.
6. Stir in salt, mace, black pepper, nutmeg, and cayenne pepper.
7. Mix in an additional 3 tablespoons of flour, stirring until well combined.
8. Cook the sauce over medium heat, stirring constantly, until it thickens and comes to a boil.
9. Add the oysters to the sauce, allowing them to heat through until they are plump and their edges curl slightly. Do not allow the mixture to boil.
10. Spoon approximately 1/2 cup of the oyster mixture into each cooked patty shell.
11. Serve immediately. This recipe serves 8 portions, with two patties in each serving.
